audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra land-Sabbath and Jubilee text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for land Sabbath, seven sabbatical cycles, Jubilee trumpet, return of property and kinship, price by harvest years, Divine provision, land redemption, houses, Levite property, poverty support, interest prohibition, Israelite debt-servitude, foreign-owned Israelite redemption, and anti-slavery safeguards.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When you come into land that I give you, the land shall keep Sabbath to YHWH.
Six years you shall sow your field, and six years prune your vineyard and gather its produce.
But in seventh year there shall be Sabbath of Sabbath-rest for land, Sabbath to YHWH; your field you shall not sow and your vineyard you shall not prune.
What grows by itself from your harvest you shall not reap, and grapes of your untrimmed vine you shall not gather; year of Sabbath-rest it shall be for land.
Sabbath produce of land shall be food for you, for your male servant and female servant, for your hired worker and for sojourner who sojourns with you,
and for your beast and wild animal in your land all its produce shall be for food.
You shall count for yourself seven Sabbaths of years, seven years seven times; days of seven Sabbaths of years shall be for you forty-nine years.
You shall cause trumpet of loud blast to pass in seventh month on tenth of month; on Day of Atonement/Coverings you shall cause trumpet to pass through all your land.

You shall sanctify fiftieth year and proclaim liberty/release in land to all its inhabitants; Jubilee it shall be for you, and you shall return each one to his property and each one to his clan shall return.
Jubilee, the fiftieth year, it shall be for you; you shall not sow and shall not reap what grows by itself, and shall not gather from untrimmed vines.
For Jubilee it is; holy it shall be to you. From field you shall eat its produce.
In this year of Jubilee each one shall return to his property.
When you sell anything to your companion or buy from hand of your companion, do not wrong one another.
According to number of years after Jubilee you shall buy from your companion; according to number of crop years he shall sell to you.
According to many years you shall increase its price, and according to few years you shall diminish its price, for number of crops he is selling to you.
You shall not wrong one another, but fear your Divine; for I am YHWH your Divine.
You shall do my statutes and keep my judgments and do them, and you shall dwell on land securely.
The land shall give its fruit, and you shall eat to satisfaction and dwell securely upon it.
If you say, What shall we eat in seventh year? Look, we shall not sow and shall not gather our produce.
I will command my blessing for you in sixth year, and it shall make produce for three years.
You shall sow eighth year and eat from old produce until ninth year; until its produce comes, you shall eat old.
The land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is mine; for sojourners and resident settlers you are with me.
In all land of your possession you shall give redemption for land.
When your brother becomes poor and sells from his possession, his nearest redeemer shall come and redeem what his brother sold.
If a man has no redeemer but his hand obtains and finds enough for its redemption,
he shall count years from its sale, return excess to man to whom he sold, and return to his possession.
But if his hand does not find enough to return to him, what he sold shall remain in hand of buyer until year of Jubilee; in Jubilee it shall go out, and he shall return to his possession.
When a man sells dwelling house in walled city, its redemption shall be until completion of year from its sale; a full year shall be its redemption.
If it is not redeemed by completion of full year, house in city with wall shall stand permanently to buyer throughout generations; it shall not go out in Jubilee.
But houses of villages with no wall around them shall be counted as field of land; redemption shall be for it, and in Jubilee it shall go out.
As for cities of Levites, houses of cities of their possession, age-long redemption shall be for Levites.
If one redeems from Levites, sold house in city of his possession shall go out in Jubilee, for houses of cities of Levites are their possession among children of Israel.
Field of pastureland of their cities shall not be sold, for age-long possession it is for them.
When your brother becomes poor and his hand falters with you, you shall strengthen him, as sojourner and resident settler, and he shall live with you.
Do not take from him interest or increase, but fear your Divine, so your brother may live with you.
Your silver you shall not give him at interest, and for increase you shall not give your food.
I am YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt, to give you land of Canaan, to be to you as Divine.
When your brother becomes poor with you and sells himself to you, you shall not make him serve service of slave.
As hired worker, as resident settler, he shall be with you; until year of Jubilee he shall serve with you.
Then he shall go out from you, he and his children with him, and return to his clan and to possession of his fathers he shall return.
For they are my servants whom I brought out from land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as sale of slaves.
You shall not rule over him with crushing severity, but fear your Divine.
Your male and female slaves whom you may have shall be from nations around you; from them you may acquire male and female slaves.
Also from children of resident settlers sojourning with you, from them you may acquire, and from their clans with you whom they fathered in your land; they may be possession for you.

You may cause them to inherit to your children after you to possess as possession; into the age you may make them serve. But over your brothers, children of Israel, one over another, you shall not rule with crushing severity.

When hand of sojourner or resident settler with you obtains wealth, and your brother with him becomes poor and sells himself to sojourner, resident settler with you, or to offspring of clan of sojourner,
after he is sold, redemption shall be for him; one of his brothers may redeem him,
or his uncle or son of his uncle may redeem him, or from close flesh of his clan may redeem him; or if his hand obtains, he may redeem himself.
He shall calculate with his buyer from year he sold himself to him until year of Jubilee, and silver of his sale shall be according to number of years; as days of hired worker shall he be with him.
If there are still many years, according to them he shall return his redemption from silver of his purchase.
If few years remain until year of Jubilee, he shall calculate for himself; according to his years he shall return his redemption.
As hired worker year by year he shall be with him; he shall not rule over him with crushing severity before your eyes.
If he is not redeemed by these, he shall go out in year of Jubilee, he and his children with him.
For to me children of Israel are servants; my servants they are whom I brought out from land of Egypt. I am YHWH your Divine.

translation_notes: Jubilee proclaims deror/liberty-release to all inhabitants and resets land and kinship displacement.
Land cannot be sold permanently because land belongs to YHWH; humans are sojourners/resident settlers with Divine.
Sale price is tied to remaining harvest years, limiting speculative permanent ownership.
When a kinsperson becomes poor, the command is strengthen him so he may live with you; interest and food-profit are prohibited.
Israelite debt-servitude is explicitly not to be treated as slave labor and must end by Jubilee.
Verses 44-46 preserve foreign slavery in the ancient source and require the strongest anti-slavery audit; they must not be normalized, defended, or treated as timeless ethics.
Exodus liberation is the chapter's governing moral claim: people liberated from Egypt belong to YHWH, not to human masters.
safety_note: Leviticus 25 must not be used to defend slavery, racial hierarchy, landlord domination, predatory lending, permanent dispossession, or debt bondage. Its strongest trajectory is land rest, return, redemption, release, anti-interest protection, and refusal of crushing rule. The foreign-slavery exception is source-preserved as a morally dangerous ancient limitation requiring explicit rejection in modern application.
